Package org.eventb.core.ast.tests

Class TestWD

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.ast.tests.AbstractTests
org.eventb.core.ast.tests.TestWD
public class TestWD extends AbstractTests
Unit and acceptance tests for the computation of WD lemmas.

    void
    testBuiltinTotalFunctionApplication()
    Shows that the WD predicate is true for the functional image through a built-in total function, and only in that case.
    void
    testCDIS()
    Acceptance test for CDIS model
    void
    testCond()
    Unit test for COND operator
    void
    testDatatype()
    Unit test for data type extensions
    void
    testDatatypeOneConstructorOnly()
    Unit test to check the simplification of the WD of a destructor when there is only one datatype constructor.
    void
    testDIR()
    Tests coming from model "DIR41.4 "
    void
    testExtensions()
    Unit test for mathematical extensions
    void
    testNotSimplified()
    Shows that some WD predicates are not simplified, although they could be.
    void
    testQuantifiedAfter()
    Ensures that WD simplification does not mess up bound identifiers, even after a quantifier.
    void
    testQuantifiedDeep()
    Ensures that WD simplification does not mess up bound identifiers, even in the presence of deep nesting.
    void
    testQuantifiedMany()
    Ensures that WD simplification does not mess up bound identifiers, even in the presence of multiple quantifiers.
    void
    testQuantifierDeepDuplicate()
    Ensures that WD conditions produced by two copies of the same predicate are properly simplified.
    void
    testQuantifiers()
    Ensures that WD simplification does not mess up bound identifiers
    void
    testRedundant()
     
    void
    testRouting()
    Tests coming from model "routing_new" from Jean-Raymond Abrial.
    void
    testSimplified()
    Ensures that documented simplification rules are indeed implemented.
    void
    testTraversal()
    Ensures that the predicates which are not part of an implication are not simplified by predicates which belong to an implication.
    void
    testWD()

    • testWD

      public void testWD()

    • testRedundant

      public void testRedundant()

    • testSimplified

      public void testSimplified()
      Ensures that documented simplification rules are indeed implemented.

    • testBuiltinTotalFunctionApplication

      public void testBuiltinTotalFunctionApplication()
      Shows that the WD predicate is true for the functional image through a built-in total function, and only in that case. Cf. FR #357: Simpler WD for built-in total functions

    • testNotSimplified

      public void testNotSimplified()
      Shows that some WD predicates are not simplified, although they could be.

    • testRouting

      public void testRouting()
      Tests coming from model "routing_new" from Jean-Raymond Abrial.

    • testDIR

      public void testDIR()
      Tests coming from model "DIR41.4 "

    • testQuantifiers

      public void testQuantifiers()
      Ensures that WD simplification does not mess up bound identifiers

    • testQuantifiedMany

      public void testQuantifiedMany()
      Ensures that WD simplification does not mess up bound identifiers, even in the presence of multiple quantifiers.

    • testQuantifiedAfter

      public void testQuantifiedAfter()
      Ensures that WD simplification does not mess up bound identifiers, even after a quantifier.

    • testQuantifiedDeep

      public void testQuantifiedDeep()
      Ensures that WD simplification does not mess up bound identifiers, even in the presence of deep nesting.

    • testQuantifierDeepDuplicate

      public void testQuantifierDeepDuplicate()
      Ensures that WD conditions produced by two copies of the same predicate are properly simplified. Also checks, that if the duplication is not exact, only sound simplifications are carried.

    • testCDIS

      public void testCDIS()
      Acceptance test for CDIS model

    • testTraversal

      public void testTraversal()
      Ensures that the predicates which are not part of an implication are not simplified by predicates which belong to an implication. Also checks, that all the isolated predicates are used to simplify implications.

    • testExtensions

      public void testExtensions()
      Unit test for mathematical extensions

    • testCond

      public void testCond()
      Unit test for COND operator

    • testDatatype

      public void testDatatype() throws Exception
      Unit test for data type extensions
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testDatatypeOneConstructorOnly

      public void testDatatypeOneConstructorOnly()
      Unit test to check the simplification of the WD of a destructor when there is only one datatype constructor.
